meta.name: Signal Edition
meta.audience: founders product leaders and launch teams
meta.memory_point: A launch website that feels like an annotated product briefing, not a generic SaaS landing page.

typography.font_body: Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, "Segoe UI", sans-serif

site_header.role: Public website orientation and brand masthead.
site_header.style_intent: Header behaves like a refined launch masthead with few links, strong brand identity, and one clear visitor action.

states.default: Masthead sits quietly above the hero while keeping brand and CTA visible.
states.hover: Links use a warm underline or text shift without moving layout.
states.focus: Keyboard focus is visible around both links and CTA.
states.sticky: Sticky behavior stays compact and never covers hero copy.
states.mobile: Mobile keeps brand and primary action visible before opening navigation.

site_header.responsive_behavior: Navigation collapses to a concise menu while brand and CTA remain reachable.
site_header.accessibility_behavior: Header uses a nav landmark, text labels, and a specific CTA label.
site_header.exceed_expectations: The header exposes launch phase and proof link, making the site feel like a real product launch.

hero.style_intent: Hero feels like an editorial cover but still shows the actual product promise and proof in the first viewport.

states.default: Cover-like typography and annotated preview make the product memorable.
states.hover: Preview areas reveal annotation affordance without decorative movement.
states.focus: CTA focus remains visible against the warm editorial background.
states.loading: Preview skeleton preserves composition while product media loads.
states.error: Preview can show a correction note instead of going blank.

hero.responsive_behavior: Hero stacks into headline, CTA, proof chips, then preview on mobile.
hero.accessibility_behavior: Hero uses one h1 and keeps proof text outside inaccessible imagery.
hero.exceed_expectations: The hero includes source-backed proof chips and an annotated product preview instead of abstract decoration.
hero.headline: Launch a website visitors remember after the first scroll.
hero.subcopy: Signal Edition turns a product idea into a composed public website with a clear story, credible proof, and sections that feel intentionally designed.
hero.composition: Annotated launch-room preview with proof captions

button.style_intent: Buttons feel deliberate and editorial, with strong contrast and restrained motion.

states.default: Primary CTA uses the warm accent as a strong editorial mark.
states.hover: Hover lifts slightly and deepens the action surface.
states.focus: Focus ring stays visible outside the button edge.
states.loading: Loading keeps width stable and reads Preparing preview.
states.disabled: Disabled action keeps readable copy and reason nearby.

button.responsive_behavior: Primary website CTAs become full-width on mobile while secondary actions sit below.
button.accessibility_behavior: CTA labels describe the actual next step and never rely on icon-only affordances.
button.exceed_expectations: Primary CTAs can use launch-specific states like Previewing, Reserved, Published, or Request received.

feature.style_intent: Feature sections read like a sequence of editorial chapters, each with proof and a clear visitor takeaway.

states.default: Feature cards use warm surfaces, chapter numbering, and concise outcome copy.
states.hover: Hover reveals a proof caption or subtle border warmth.
states.focus: Linked features have visible focus without changing layout.
states.loading: Preview media keeps section rhythm while assets load.

feature.responsive_behavior: Feature cards stack into a deliberate story order on mobile.
feature.accessibility_behavior: Feature cards use headings and text proof; icons are decorative only.
feature.exceed_expectations: Each feature has a visitor outcome plus a proof detail, not just a capability label.
feature.section_headline: A website system with a point of view.
feature.section_subcopy: The style pack does more than swap colors. It turns the chosen aesthetic into section-specific behavior, proof, and responsive decisions.
feature.feature_one_detail: The first viewport names the offer, shows proof chips, and previews the actual website structure.
feature.feature_two_detail: Every feature, proof, testimonial, and CTA section answers a specific visitor question.
feature.feature_three_detail: Buttons, cards, pricing, FAQ, and footer adapt to the same editorial direction without becoming identical.

proof.style_intent: Proof appears as cited evidence, not a loose logo wall or fake KPI band.

states.default: Proof points include source or context so they feel credible.
states.hover: Linked proof points reveal case-study or source direction.
states.focus: Linked proof has visible focus treatment.
states.loading: Proof strip preserves spacing while logos or metrics load.
states.empty: Missing proof invites adding a source instead of showing fake numbers.

proof.responsive_behavior: Proof items stack into readable source-backed evidence on narrow screens.
proof.accessibility_behavior: Logo and metric proof always includes text context.
proof.exceed_expectations: Each proof point has a context caption, making it usable in a real launch page.
proof.source_note: Use source, segment, and timeframe so visitors know why each claim matters.

testimonial.role: Human credibility for the website offer.
testimonial.style_intent: Testimonials feel like sourced editorial pull quotes with context and result.

states.default: Quote is large enough to be memorable but short enough to scan.
states.hover: Case-study link can reveal a subtle source affordance.
states.focus: Linked testimonial has visible focus treatment.
states.loading: Attribution and quote skeleton preserve line length.

testimonial.responsive_behavior: Quote stacks above attribution on mobile with readable line length.
testimonial.accessibility_behavior: Quote text is real text and attribution is explicit.
testimonial.exceed_expectations: The quote includes a before/after result instead of generic praise.
testimonial.quote: The first version finally felt like a real launch page with a story visitors could repeat.
testimonial.attribution: Mira Chen, product lead for a seed-stage AI tool
testimonial.result: From generic SaaS to launch-ready narrative in one pass

pricing.role: Website package comparison and buying confidence.
pricing.style_intent: Pricing feels like choosing an edition, with fit cues and tradeoffs visible before the visitor clicks.

states.default: Plans use edition language and clear fit cues.
states.hover: Plan CTAs emphasize action without turning cards into ads.
states.focus: Plan links and buttons have visible focus.
states.selected: Recommended plan uses label and structure, not color alone.
states.disabled: Unavailable plan explains contact or approval path.

pricing.responsive_behavior: Plans stack on mobile with fit cues before feature detail.
pricing.accessibility_behavior: Recommended labels and price scopes are visible text.
pricing.exceed_expectations: Each plan names the visitor type and tradeoff instead of listing generic features.
pricing.section_headline: Pick the depth of website direction.
pricing.section_subcopy: Use the same style pack system for a quick launch page or a full brand site.
pricing.plan_one_detail: One-page structure, core style pack, and hero proof direction.
pricing.plan_two_detail: Full website sections, proof strategy, pricing, FAQ, and CTA behavior.
pricing.plan_three_detail: Reusable section library, visual directions, and quality gates.

faq.style_intent: FAQ answers should reduce real visitor uncertainty instead of repeating marketing claims.

states.default: Questions are specific and answers stay concise.
states.hover: Interactive rows reveal affordance without noisy decoration.
states.expanded: Expanded answers preserve comfortable reading rhythm.

faq.responsive_behavior: FAQ collapses to one column on mobile with clear spacing.
faq.accessibility_behavior: FAQ controls expose expanded state in real implementations.
faq.exceed_expectations: Each answer removes one real buying or implementation objection.
faq.section_subcopy: The system forces specificity before visual polish.
faq.a1: No. It gives an AI agent a stronger design process so the first artifact is more useful.
faq.q2: Can the same template create different sites?
faq.a2: Yes. Structure is stable, but style pack decisions change typography, sections, proof, and component behavior.
faq.a3: It prioritizes visitor story, proof, conversion, and brand memory before visual decoration.
faq.a4: Add new website sections, aesthetic directions, or style packs without rewriting the core skill.

cta.style_intent: CTA section should close the story with a concrete action and a risk reducer.

states.default: CTA uses strong contrast and a single dominant action.
states.hover: CTA button hover strengthens confidence without extra visual noise.
states.focus: Primary action focus remains visible on dark inverse band.
states.loading: Lead action preserves width and confirms progress.

cta.responsive_behavior: CTA text and action stack on mobile with the action full width.
cta.accessibility_behavior: CTA label names the next step and risk reducer is readable text.
cta.exceed_expectations: CTA includes a risk reducer so conversion feels safe, not pushy.
cta.headline: Design the website before you decorate it.
cta.subcopy: Generate a style pack, section strategy, and HTML page that all point at the same visitor story.

footer.style_intent: Footer should close the page like an editorial note while preserving useful links.

states.default: Footer groups links calmly and reinforces brand memory.
states.hover: Links show clear hover without heavy decoration.
states.focus: Footer links have visible focus state.
states.submitted: Newsletter or contact action confirms the next step.

footer.responsive_behavior: Footer stacks brand statement above grouped links on mobile.
footer.accessibility_behavior: Footer links are real text links with clear labels.
footer.exceed_expectations: Footer includes a confidence detail such as response time, open-source path, or support promise.
footer.brand_statement: A website-first HTML design skill for section-level style systems.

card.role: Website content grouping and visual rhythm.
card.style_intent: Cards act as editorial plates for proof and outcomes, not interchangeable content boxes.

states.default: Cards use warm surface, fine border, and generous rhythm.
states.hover: Linked cards gain subtle border warmth.
states.focus: Linked cards show visible focus treatment.
states.loading: Card skeleton preserves headline and caption rhythm.

card.responsive_behavior: Cards stack in story order and keep headings readable.
card.accessibility_behavior: Card titles use headings and linked cards expose real links.
card.exceed_expectations: Cards include source, result, or visitor takeaway instead of filler copy.
card.status_detail: Each card carries a visitor takeaway or proof caption.

form.role: Website lead capture or contact input.
form.style_intent: Forms are short, refined, and confidence-building for public visitors.

states.default: Fields use warm paper surfaces and clear labels.
states.focus: Focused fields use accent border and visible ring.
states.invalid: Validation states expected format in plain language.
states.success: Success confirms when visitors will hear back.

form.responsive_behavior: Forms stack on mobile and keep submit action near fields.
form.accessibility_behavior: Labels are visible and errors are text, not color only.
form.exceed_expectations: Lead forms include response time or next-step expectation.
form.safety_detail: Replies are reviewed within one business day.

quality_gates: The generated artifact is a public website with visitor-facing sections and conversion paths.
Hero, features, proof, testimonial, pricing, FAQ, CTA, and footer all express the same aesthetic direction.
The memory point is visible in the hero preview, proof captions, and CTA language.
Every website section has a visitor-facing job and specific copy.
Mobile preserves headline, CTA, product preview, proof, pricing, and footer navigation.
